author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXIII. 29, 30.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Woe unto you, because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish their sepulchers,and say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not because they build, nor because they blame the others, doth He say, woe, but because, while both thus, and by what they say, they are pretending to condemn their fathers, they do worse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that the condemnation was a pretense, Luke saith, ye do allow because ye build; for, “Woe unto you,” saith He, “for ye build the sepulchers of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Truly ye bear witness, and ye allow the deeds of your fathers, for they indeed killed them, and ye build their sepulchers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For here He reproves their purpose, wherewith they built, that it was not for the honoring of them that were slain, but as making a show of the murders, and afraid, lest, when the tombs had perished by time, the proof and memory of such daring should fade away, setting up these glorious buildings, as a kind of trophy, and priding themselves in the daring deeds of those men, and displaying them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the things that ye now dare to do, show that ye do these things also in this spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, though ye speak the contrary, saith He, as condemning them, as, for instance, “We should not, if we had been in their days, have been partakers with them;” yet the disposition is evident wherewith ye say these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also unfolding it, though darkly, still He hath expressed it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He had said, ye say, “If we had been in the days of our fathers, we should not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets;” He added, “Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them that slew the prophets.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what blame is it to be a murderer’s son, if one partake not in the mind of one’s father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is evident, that for this same thing He brings it forward against them, hinting at their affinity in wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is manifest too by what comes after; He adds at least, “Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as those beasts are like their parents, in the destructiveness of their venom, so also are ye like your fathers in murderousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, because He was searching their temper of mind, which is to the more part obscure, He doth, from those things also which they were about to perpetrate, which would be manifest to all, establish His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, because He had said, “Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets,” making it evident, that of their affinity in wickedness He is speaking, and that it was a pretense to say, “We should not have been partakers with them,” He added, “Fill ye up therefore the measure of your fathers,”not commanding, but declaring beforehand, what was to be, that is, His own murder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, having brought in their refutation, and having shown that they were pretenses which they said in their own defense, as, for instance, “We would not have been partakers with them,” (for they who refrain not from the Lord, how should they have refrained from the servants), He makes after this His language more condemnatory,calling them “serpents, and generation of vipers,” and saying, “How shall ye escape the damnation of hell,”at once perpetrating such things, and denying them, and dissembling your purpose?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then rebuking them more exceedingly from another cause also, He saith, “I will send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes, and some of them shall ye kill and crucify, and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that they should not say, “Though we crucified the Lord, yet from the servants we should have refrained, if we had been then;” “Behold,” He saith, “I send servants also to you, prophets likewise themselves, and neither will ye spare them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things He saith, showing that it was nothing strange, that He should be murdered by those sons, being both murderous and deceitful, and having much guile, and surpassing their fathers in their outrages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides what hath been said, He shows them to be also exceedingly vainglorious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they say, “If we had been in the days of our fathers, we should not have been partakers with them,” they spake out of vainglory, and were practising virtue in words only, but in their works doing the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, that is, wicked sons of wicked men, and more wicked than those who begat them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He showeth that they are committing greater crimes, both by their committing them after those others, and by their doing much more grievous things than they, and this, while positively affirming that they never would have fallen into the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they add that which is both the end and the crown of their evil deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the others slew them that came to the vineyard, but these, both the son, and them that were bidding them to the wedding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things He saith, to separate them off from the affinity to Abraham, and to show that they had no advantage from thence, unless they followed his works; wherefore also He adds, “How can ye fleefrom the damnation of hell,” when following them that have committed such acts?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here He recalls to their remembrance John’s accusation, for he too called them by this name, and reminded them of the judgment to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, because they are nothing alarmed by judgment and hell, by reason of their not believing them, and because the thing is future, He awes them by the things present, and saith, “Wherefore, behold, I send unto you prophets and scribes: and some of them shall ye kill and crucify, and scourge;that upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel, unto the blood of Zacharias the son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the templeand the altar.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Verily I say unto you, that all these things shall come upon this generation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See by how many things He has warned them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said, Ye condemn your fathers, in that ye say, “We would not have been partakers with them;” and this is no little thing to shame them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said, While ye condemn them, ye do worse things, even ye yourselves; and this is sufficient to cover them with disgrace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said, These things shall not be without punishment; and hence he implants in them fear beyond words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He hath reminded them at least of hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then because that was to come, He brought home to them the terrors as even present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For all these things shall come,” He saith, “upon this generation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He added also unspeakable severity to the vengeance, saying, that they shall suffer more grievous things than all; yet by none of these things did they become better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any one say, And why do they suffer more grievously than all? we would say, Because they have first committed more grievous things than all, and by none of the things that have been done to them have they been brought to a sound mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Heardest thou not Lamech saying, “Of Lamech vengeance shall be taken seventy times sevenfold;”that is, “I am deserving of more punishment than Cain.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why could this be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet he did not slay his brother; but because not even by his example was he brought to a better mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is what God saith elsewhere, “Requiting the sins of fathers upon children for the third and fourth generation of them that hate me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not as though one were to suffer punishment for the crimes committed by others, but inasmuch as they who, after many sin and have been punished, yet have not grown better, but have committed the same offenses, are justly worthy to suffer their punishments also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see how seasonably he also mentioned Abel, indicating that this murder likewise is of envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then have ye to say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Know ye not what Cain suffered?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did God hold His peace at his deeds?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did He not exact the severest penalty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Heard ye not what things your fathers suffered, when they slew the prophets; were they not delivered over to punishments, and inflictions of vengeance without number?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then did ye not become better?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I speak of the punishments of your fathers, and what they suffered?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou who thyself condemnest thy fathers, how is it thou doest worse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For moreover even ye yourselves have declared that “He will miserably destroy those wicked men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What favor then will ye have after this, committing such things after such a sentence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But who is this Zacharias?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Some say, the father of John; some, the prophet; some, a priest with two different names, whom the Scripture also calls, the son of Jehoiada.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou mark this, that the outrage was twofold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not only did they slay holy men, but also in a holy place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And saying these things, He did not only alarm them, but also comfort His disciples, showing that the righteous men also who were before them suffered these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these He alarmed, foretelling that like as they paid their penalty, even so should these too suffer the utmost extremities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He calls them“prophets, and wise men, and scribes,” even hereby again taking away every plea of theirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For ye cannot say,” He saith, “Thou didst send from among the Gentiles, and therefore we were offended;” but they were led on unto this by being murderous, and thirsting for blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He also said beforehand, “For this cause do I send prophets and scribes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This did the prophets also lay to their charge, saying, “They mingle blood with blood,”and that they are men of blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also did He command the blood to be offered to Him, showing that if in a brute it be thus precious, much more in a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which He saith to Noah likewise, “I will require all blood that is shed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And ten thousand other such things might one find Him enjoining with regard to their not committing murder; wherefore He commanded them not even to eat that which was strangled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Oh the love of God towards man! that though He foreknew they would profit nothing, He still doeth His part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I will send, He saith, and this knowing they would be slain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that even hereby they were convicted of saying vainly, “We should not have been partakers with our fathers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these too slew prophets even in their synagogues, and reverenced neither the place, nor the dignity of the persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not merely ordinary persons did they slay, but prophets and wise men, such that they had nothing to lay to their charge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And by these He meaneth the apostles, and those after them, for, indeed, many prophesied.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, willing to aggravate their fears, He saith, “Verily, verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation;” that is, I will bring all upon your heads, and will make the vengeance sore.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that knew many to have sinned, and was not sobered, but himself hath committed the same sins again, and not the same only, but also far more grievous, would justly deserve to suffer a far more grievous punishment than they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as, if he had been minded, he would have gained greatly, had he grown better by their examples, even so, since he continued without amendment, he is liable to a heavier vengeance, as having had the benefit of more warning by them who had sinned before and been punished, and having reaped no advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He directs His speech unto the city, in this way too being minded to correct His hearers, and saith, “O Jerusalem, Jerusalem!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What meaneth the repetition? this is the manner of one pitying her, and bemoaning her, and greatly loving her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, like as unto a woman beloved, herself indeed ever loved, but who had despised Him that loved her, and therefore on the point of being punished, He pleads, being now about to inflict the punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which He doth in the prophets also, using these words, “I said, Turn thou unto me, and she returned not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then having called her, He tells also her blood-stained deeds, “Thou that killestthe prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, and ye would not,” in this way also pleading for His own dealings; not even with these things hast thou turned me aside, nor withdrawn me from my great affection toward thee, but it was my desire even so, not once or twice, but often to draw thee unto me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For how often would I have gathered your children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens, and ye would not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He saith, to show that they were ever scattering themselves by their sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And His affection He indicates by the similitude; for indeed the creature is warm in its love towards its brood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And everywhere in the prophets is this same image of the wings, and in the song of Moses and in the Psalms, indicating His great protection and care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But ye would not,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Behold your house is left desolate,”stripped of the succor which cometh from me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Surely it was the same, who also was before protecting them, and holding them together, and preserving them; surely it was He who was ever chastening them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He appoints a punishment, which they had ever dreaded exceedingly; for it declared the entire overthrow of their polity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is the language of one that loves earnestly, earnestly drawing them unto Him by the things to come, not merely warning them by the past; for of the future day of His second coming doth He here speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did they not see Him from that time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But it is not that hour which He meaneth in saying, Henceforth, but the time up to His crucifixion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since they were forever accusing Him of this, that He was a kind of rival God, and a foe to God, He moves them to love Him by this, namely, by showing Himself to be of one accord with His Father; and He indicates Himself to be the same that was in the prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He uses the same words as did the prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And by these He intimated both His resurrection, and His second coming, and made it plain even to the utterly unbelieving, that then most surely they should worship Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how did He make this plain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By speaking of many things that were first to be, that He should send prophets, that they should kill them; that it should be in the synagogues; that they should suffer the utmost extremities; that their house should be left desolate; that they should undergo things more grievous than any, and such as never were undergone before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all these things are enough to furnish even to the most senseless and contentious a clear proof of that which should come to pass at His coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I will ask them, Did He send the prophets and wise men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did they slay them in their synagogue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Was their house left desolate?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did all the vengeance come upon that generation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is quite plain that it was so, and no man gainsays it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As then all these things came to pass, so shall those also come to pass, and most surely they shall submit then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they shall derive thence no advantage in the way of defense, as neither will they who repent of their course of life then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore let us, while it is time, practise what is good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as they henceforth derived no advantage from their knowledge, even so neither shall we ourselves from our repentance for our wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither to the pilot, when the bark is sunk in the sea from his remissness, will there remain anything more; nor to the physician, when the sick man is gone; but each of these must before the end devise, and execute all things, so as to be involved in no danger, nor shame; but after this, all is unprofitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us also then, while in sickness, send for physicians, and lay out money, and exert unceasing diligence, that having risen up from our affliction, we may depart hence in health.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as much care as we exert about our servants, when their bodies are sick, so much let us show forth upon ourselves, when our soul is diseased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed we are nearer to ourselves than our servants, and our souls are more necessary than those bodies, but nevertheless it were well if we exert at least an equal diligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we do not this now, when gone, thenceforth we may obtain nothing more in the way of plea.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who is so wretched, one may say, as not to show even as much thought as this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why this is the marvellous thing, that we are held in so little esteem with our ownselves, that we despise ourselves more than our servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when our servants are sick of a fever, we send for physicians, and make a separation in the house, and compel them to obey the laws of that art; and if these are neglected, we are displeased with them, and set persons to watch them, who will not, even should they wish them, suffer them to satiate their desire; and if they who have the care of these persons should say, that medicines must be procured at great cost, we yield; and whatsoever they may enjoin, we obey, and we pay them hire for these injunctions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when we are sick (or rather there is no time when we are not sick), we do not so much as call in the physician, we do not lay out money, but as though some ruffian,and enemy, and foes were concerned, so do we disregard our soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things I say, not finding fault with our attention towards our servants, but thinking it meet to take at least as much care of our souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how should we do? one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Show it to Paul when ill; call in Matthew; let John sit by it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear from them, what we ought to do that is thus ill, they will surely tell, and will not conceal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they are not dead, but live and speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But doth the soul take no heed to them, being weighed down by the fever?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou compel it, and awaken its reasoning power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Call in the prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no need to pay money to these physicians, for neither do they themselves demand hire for themselves, nor for the medicines which they prepare do they drive thee to the necessity of expense, except for almsgiving; but in everything else they even add to thy possessions; as, for instance, when they require thee to be temperate, they deliver thee from unseasonable and wrong expenses; when they tell thee to abstain from drunkenness, they make thee wealthier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the skill of physicians, who besides health, are supplying thee also with riches?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Sit down therefore by them, and learn of them the nature of thy disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For instance, dost thou love wealth, and greedy gain, like as the fevered love water?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Listen at any rate to their admonitions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as the physician saith to thee, If thou wilt gratify thy desire, thou wilt perish, and undergo this or that; even so also Paul: “They that will be rich, fall into temptation, and a snare of the devil, and into foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But art thou impatient?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear him saying, “Yet a little while, and He that cometh will come, and will not tarry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The Lord is at hand, be careful for nothing;”and again, “The fashion of this world passeth away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither doth he command only, but also soothes, as a physician should.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as they devise some other things in the place of cold things, so doth this man draw offthe desire another way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to be rich, saith he; let it be “in good works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou desire to lay up treasure?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I forbid it not at all; only let it be in Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as the physician saith, that what is cold is hurtful to teeth, to nerves, to bones; so he too, more briefly indeed, as heedful of brevity, yet far more, clearly and more powerfully, saith, “For the love of money is the root of all evils.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of what then should one make use?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He tells this also: of contentedness instead of covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For contentment,” he saith, “with godliness is great gain.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou art dissatisfied, and desirest more, and art not yet equal to cast away all superfluous things, he tells also him that is thus diseased, how he ought to handle these things too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “That they that rejoice in wealth, be as though they rejoiced not; and they that have, as though they possessed not; and they that use this world, as not abusing it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou what manner of things he enjoins?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou call in also another physician besides?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To me at least it seems well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither are these physicians like those of the body, who often, while vying one with another, overwhelm the sick man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not so these, for they have regard to the health of the sick, not to their own vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be not then afraid of the number of them; one Master speaks in all, that is, Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See, for instance, another again entering in, and saying severe things concerning this disease, or rather it is the Master by him;“For ye cannot serve God and mammon.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, saith he, and how will these things be? how shall we cease from the desire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence may we learn this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how shall we know?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear him saying this too: “Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon the earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how by the place, by the things that waste there, He draws men off from this desire that is here, and rivets them to Heaven, where all things are impregnable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if ye transfer your wealth there where neither rust nor moth corrupts, nor thieves break through and steal, ye will both expel this disease, and establish your soul in the greatest abundance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with what we have said, He brings forward an example also to teach thee moderation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as the physician, to alarm the sick man saith, that such a one died from the use of cold water; so doth He also bring in the rich man,laboring indeed, and longing for life and health, but not able to attain thereto, because of having set his heart on covetousness, but going away empty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides this man, another is shown to thee again by another evangelist, he that was in torment,and was not master so much as of a drop of water.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then showing that His injunctions are easy, He saith, “Behold the fowls of the air.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But being compassionate, He suffers not even the rich to despair.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the things which are impossible with men, are possible with God,”saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though thou be rich, the physician is able to cure thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither was it wealth that He took away, but to be slave of riches, and a lover of greedy gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then is it possible for the rich man to be saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By possessing his goods in common with them that are in need, being such as Job was, and exterminating out of his soul the desire of more, and in no points going beyond real need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He shows thee together with these this selfsame publican also, that was grievously oppressed by the fever of covetousness, quickly set free from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what more sordid than a publican?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, the man became indifferent to wealth from obeying the laws of the physician.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed He hath for His disciples such persons as these, that were sick of the same diseases as we are, and have recovered their health quickly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He shows us each, in order we may not despair.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at least this publican.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark again another, a chief of the publicans, who promised four fold indeed for all that he had extorted, and the half of all that he possessed, that he might receive Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But art thou on fire with exceeding desire for riches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Have the possessions of all men instead of thine own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed I give thee, He saith, more than thou seekest, in opening to thee the houses of the wealthy throughout the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For whosoever hath forsaken father or mother, or lands, or house, shall receive an hundredfold.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus wilt thou not enjoy more abundant possessions only, but thou wilt even remove this grievous thirst altogether, and wilt endure all things easily, so far from desiring more, not seeking often even necessary things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus doth Paul suffer hunger, and is held in honor more than when he ate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Forasmuch as a wrestler also, when striving, and winning crowns, would not choose to give up and to be in repose; and a merchant who hath entered on sea voyages would not desire to be afterwards in idleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And we therefore, if we should taste as we ought of spiritual fruits, shall thenceforth not even account the things present to be anything, being seized by the desire of the things to come as with some most noble intoxication.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us taste of them, therefore, that we may both be delivered from the turmoil of the things present, and may attain the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory and the might, now and ever, and world without end.
